summary: Small cross-cutting cleanup PR motivated by removing M2C_FIELD usage while improving matches in fighter action/collision/material code, common fighter states, sword item code, and lb refract headers. The changes replaced raw offset and bit-mask access with named struct fields, bitfields, GET_FIGHTER/GET_ITEM helpers, typed command-script access, and clearer loop/control-flow shapes where those helped matching.
what_changed: Replaced several raw fighter field accesses with named fields, e.g. ftCo_DamageFall now checks fp->ground_or_air == GA_Ground instead of M2C_FIELD(fp, s32*, 0xE0).

Simplified ftCo_Guard setup by using GET_FIGHTER(gobj), named guard motion variables such as fp->mv.co.guard.x14/x18/x1C, and named fighter bitfields x221C_b1/x221C_b2/x221C_b3 instead of raw 0x221C masks.
Added a typed union view to FtCmdState::x8 in src/melee/ft/types.h, exposing x8_bits with signed halfword fields x0/x2 and byte-level bitfields x0_b0..x3_b7 for command-script decoding.

Cleaned ftAction_8007121C hitbox command parsing by replacing multiple M2C_FIELD reads and manual masks with HitCapsule fields/bitfields such as hit->jobj, x40_b0..x40_b3, x41_b4..x41_b7, x42_b0/x42_b1/x42_b2/x42_b4/x42_b5/x42_b7, x43_b0/x43_b1/x43_b2, and hit_grabbed_victim_only.

Restructured ftAction_8007349C from goto-style command-script loop control into early return plus a do/while loop, while using cmd->u.Command_09->id and ftAction_803C0870[id - 10] for command advancement.

Rewrote ftColl_8007B0C0 from pointer walking and raw x221A bit manipulation into a for loop over fp->hurt_capsules, setting state and skip_update_pos, then assigning fp->x221A_b5 based on HurtCapsule_Enabled.
Expanded lb HitCapsule layout in src/melee/lb/types.h by replacing the anonymous padding byte at +0x41 with named bitfields x41_b0 through x41_b7.
Updated ftMaterial_800BF2B8 to call lbRefract_80022998 with an additional s8 argument from fp->smash_attrs.x2134_vibrateFrame and updated the lbrefract.h prototype accordingly.
Updated ftMaterial_800BF534 signature to accept rendermode and adjusted its declaration/call site.
Replaced a raw virtual-method call through mobj->parent.class_info offset 0x48 with HSD_MOBJ_METHOD(mobj)->setup_tev(mobj, tobj, mobj_rendermode).

Cleaned sword item code by removing redundant spawn.pos assignments, correcting repeated item-var references in it_80285084/it_80285140 to the local x10/x14/x18 and x28/x2C/x30 groups, and simplifying it_802852B8 arithmetic.

Changed ftCo_AirCatch_Anim from a ternary expression to an if/else assignment for var_f3; the visible expressions are equivalent in the excerpt, so this appears to be a control-flow/codegen matching tweak rather than a semantic change.
smart_moves: Added typed command byte/bit access in FtCmdState instead of repeatedly decoding the same command stream through M2C_FIELD; this localizes script-layout knowledge and makes later command parsing cleaner.
Promoted known byte bits in HitCapsule and Fighter to named bitfields, allowing assignments like hit->x42_b5 = 1 or fp->x221A_b5 = false to replace brittle mask/or/and sequences.
Used existing accessor macros such as GET_FIGHTER and GET_ITEM where appropriate, reducing cast noise and making decompiled code align with project style.
Converted raw class-info function-pointer access to HSD_MOBJ_METHOD(...)->setup_tev, preserving behavior while documenting the dispatch slot through the HSD material vtable abstraction.

Retained matching-sensitive dummy stack arrays where needed, e.g. ftAction_8007121C still has a local u8 padding array, but adjusted its size, showing cleanup was balanced against stack/codegen constraints.
Rewrote goto-heavy command execution logic into structured control flow without erasing matching details such as F32_MAX timer semantics and command table advancement.
decomp_lessons: When a raw M2C_FIELD offset has a known meaning, prefer adding or using the correct struct field/bitfield over leaving offset casts in function bodies.
For command-script readers, a union overlay can be a practical decomp tactic: keep the raw pointer for byte advancement while exposing typed halfword and bitfield views for individual command fields.
Bitfield naming pays off immediately in collision/action code because many match-blocking statements are just set/clear operations on packed state bytes.
Header/prototype cleanup is part of matching: call-site fixes in ftmaterial required corresponding updates in ftmaterial.h and lbrefract.h.
Structured loops can match better than m2c-style gotos when the underlying control flow is understood; ftAction_8007349C demonstrates early exits plus do/while around command execution.

Redundant-looking source changes, such as splitting an equivalent ternary into if/else in ftCo_AirCatch_Anim, may be intentional codegen-shape adjustments and should not be dismissed if they improve matching.
Replacing pointer-walk loops with indexed for loops is safe when array length and element type are established, and improves readability while maintaining semantics.
naming_conventions: Fighter packed flags use offset-plus-bit names such as x221A_b5, x221C_b1, x221C_b2, x221C_b3, x2223_b2, and x2223_b7.
HitCapsule packed flags use offset-plus-bit names such as x40_b0, x41_b4, x42_b5, and x43_b2, with semantic aliases where known such as hit_grabbed_victim_only.
Unknown movement subfields remain under mode-specific namespaces, e.g. fp->mv.co.guard.x14, x18, and x1C.
Command byte overlays in FtCmdState use x8_bits plus byte/bit names x0_b0 through x3_b7 and signed halfword names x0 and x2.
Project accessor macros GET_FIGHTER(gobj) and GET_ITEM(gobj) are preferred over direct gobj->user_data casts when available.
HSD vtable access should use method macros such as HSD_MOBJ_METHOD(mobj)->setup_tev rather than raw class_info offset calls when the method is identified.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed many M2C_FIELD and M2C_BITWISE-style artifacts by introducing named fields and bitfields, which can improve both readability and compiler codegen stability.
Used typed signed and unsigned halfword reads from command data to avoid extra casts, e.g. M2C_FIELD(cmd->x8, u16*, 0), M2C_FIELD(cmd->x8, s16*, 2), and cmd->x8_bits->x0/x2.
Maintained local stack padding arrays in functions where stack layout likely affects matching.
Changed ftCo_AirCatch_Anim expression shape from ternary initialization to separate declaration plus if/else assignment, likely to influence emitted branch/register structure.
Recast ftAction_8007349C command loop to avoid labels/gotos while preserving timer break conditions and repeated execution until cmd->timer returns to F32_MAX.
Moved from byte mask updates to direct bitfield assignments, causing source-level operations to reflect intended single-bit stores/updates.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue replacing M2C_FIELD in ftAction command parsing.
Search for additional FtCmdState x8_bits uses to verify the command overlay naming pattern remained stable.
Search for later HitCapsule bitfield renames from x40_b*/x41_b*/x42_b*/x43_b* to semantic names.
Look for PRs touching lbRefract_80022998 or ftMaterial_800BF534 to see whether the new s8/rendermode parameters were later named.
Find follow-up cleanups in it/items/itsword.c around xDD4_itemVar.sword field names.
